---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- HERO SECTION -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

::: {.hero}

::: {.columns .v-center}

:::: {.column width="60%"}

# Advancing quantum sensing through solid-state spin defects, precision instrumentation, and scientific software.

## Amandeep Singh

**Experimental Quantum Physicist**

Postdoctoral Fellow  
Institute of Applied Physics  
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem

<div class="hero-buttons">

[Research](pages/research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Projects](pages/projects.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Software](pages/software.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Publications](pages/publications.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[CV](pages/cv.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

</div>

::::

:::: {.column width="40%"}

![](assets/images/profile/profile.jpg){.profile-photo fig-alt="Amandeep Singh"}

::::

:::

:::

# Research Vision

I am an experimental quantum physicist developing next-generation quantum sensing technologies based on solid-state spin defects. My research combines **quantum sensing**, **quantum magnetic imaging**, **precision instrumentation**, **quantum control**, **scientific software**, and **nanofabrication** to create experimental platforms for quantum science and emerging quantum technologies.

My long-term research vision is to develop scalable quantum sensing platforms that bridge fundamental quantum physics with practical technologies for materials science, condensed matter physics, and biomedical applications. By integrating precision instrumentation, advanced quantum control, and scientific software, I aim to build experimental systems that enable new scientific discoveries and real-world quantum technologies.

---

# Research Pillars

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.research-card}

## ⚛ Quantum Sensing

Developing quantum sensors based on solid-state spin defects for precision magnetic field sensing and metrology.

**Key Technologies**

- Diamond NV Centers
- ODMR
- Quantum Magnetometry

[Explore →](pages/research.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.research-card}

## 🧲 Quantum Magnetic Imaging

Developing widefield imaging techniques for mapping magnetic fields with high spatial and temporal resolution.

**Key Technologies**

- Widefield ODMR
- Microwave-Free Imaging
- Event Cameras

[Explore →](pages/research.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.research-card}

## ⚡ Quantum Control

Building experimental control systems for synchronized quantum experiments.

**Key Technologies**

- Quantum Machines OPX
- Pulse Engineering
- Experimental Automation

[Explore →](pages/research.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-6}

::: {.research-card}

## 💻 Scientific Software

Designing modular software frameworks for experimental control, automation, and scientific data analysis.

**Key Technologies**

- NV Control Framework
- Python
- Hardware Integration

[Explore →](pages/software.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-6}

::: {.research-card}

## 🔬 Nanofabrication

Developing quantum devices through advanced microfabrication and materials characterization.

**Key Technologies**

- Diamond Processing
- Device Fabrication
- Materials Characterization

[Explore →](pages/research.qmd)

:::

:::

:::

---

# Featured Projects

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.project-card}

<span class="status-active">● Active Development</span>

## NV Control Framework

A modular Python framework for controlling and automating diamond NV center experiments with a scalable architecture for future instrumentation.

**Technologies**

Python • PySide6 • VISA • Quantum Machines • Pulse Streamer

[Explore Project →](projects/nv-control/index.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.project-card}

<span class="status-active">● Active Research</span>

## Microwave-Free Quantum Magnetic Imaging

Developing microwave-free magnetic imaging using high-density NV ensembles for robust quantum sensing.

**Technologies**

Widefield ODMR • High-PPM Diamond • Imaging

[Explore Project →](pages/projects.qmd)

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}

::: {.project-card}

<span class="status-active">● Active Research</span>

## Event Camera Quantum Imaging

Developing ultrafast quantum magnetic imaging using neuromorphic event-based vision sensors.

**Technologies**

Event Camera • Widefield NV • High-Speed Imaging

[Explore Project →](pages/projects.qmd)

:::

:::

:::

---

# Selected Publications

*A curated selection of representative publications will appear here.*

[View All Publications →](pages/publications.qmd)

---

# Get in Touch

If you are interested in collaboration, scientific discussions, open-source software, or quantum sensing research, please feel free to get in touch.

[Contact →](pages/contact.qmd)
